wordplay_note: A host whose world no longer hosts him — erased from all records
card_description: Jason Taverner (meaning "Taverner = innkeeper / host") is a character in Philip K. Dick's *Flow My Tears the Policeman Said*. Famous TV entertainer who wakes up in a world where no one knows he exists. Name note: A host whose world no longer hosts him — erased from all records. Celebrity and identity as social constructs — without records you cease to exist. Appears in 3 Exegesis passages. First appears in *Flow My Tears the Policeman Said*. Context: "Dick argues that *Flow My Tears* contains "cooked" (divinely inspired/interpolated) material that parallels the Book of Acts with impossible precision."
first_work: Flow My Tears the Policeman Said
work_list: Flow My Tears the Policeman Said
